manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: underwear, cedar, stairs, sapphire, satin, mile, mint, nose, toe.
Your opponent's team words are: rosemary, opal, blouse, yard, tomato, cotton, basement, nylon.
Distractor words are: floor, orange, onyx, sage, melon, velvet, inch.
The kill words are: lobby.

The goal is to link as many words as possible without being too vague or touching opponent/killer words.

- **toe, nose**: Body parts.

- Distractors: None really.
- Opponents: None.

reasoning_details.text:
The user wants me to play the role of a "spymaster" in the game Codenames.
I need to provide a single-word clue that relates to some of my team's words.
The goal is to link as many words as possible without being too vague or touching opponent/killer words.

My team's words: underwear, cedar, stairs, sapphire, satin, mile, mint, nose, toe.
Opponent's words: rosemary, opal, blouse, yard, tomato, cotton, basement, nylon.
Distractors: floor, orange, onyx, sage, melon, velvet, inch.
Kill word: lobby.

Let's analyze my team's words to find connections:
- **underwear, satin**: Satin is a fabric often used in underwear (or lingerie).
- **cedar, nose, mint**: Smells. Cedar smells woody, nose smells, mint is a scent.
- **stairs, toe**: You stub your toe on stairs? Or just body parts/actions.
- **sapphire, nose**: Sapphire is blue, nose is... not really.
- **mile, yard**: Distances, but "yard" is an opponent word.
- **underwear, nose**: Not really.
- **toe, nose**: Body parts.

Let's look for potential 2-word or 3-word combinations.

Option 1: **Scent** or **Smell**
- Target: **cedar** (cedar scent), **nose** (organ of smell), **mint** (minty scent).
- Opponents/Distractors check: Rosemary (opponent) smells, Sage (distractor) smells.
- "Rosemary" is a strong herb scent. "Sage" is also a scent. This might be risky because there are multiple smelling words on the board.

Option 2: **Body**
- Target: **nose**, **toe**.
- Distractors: None really.
- Opponents: None.
- This is safe, but only 2 words.

Option 3: **Blue**
- Target: **sapphire**.
- Distractors: **onyx**? No. **velvet**? No.
- Opponents: None.
- Only 1 word. Not efficient.

Option 4: **Fabric**
- Target: **underwear** (made of fabric), **satin** (a fabric).
- Opponents: **blouse**, **cotton**, **nylon**. All relate to fabric/clothing.
- Too risky.

Option 5: **Stone** or **Gem**
- Target: **sapphire**.
- Distractors: **onyx**.
- Opponents: **opal**.
- Too risky.

Option 6: **Walking**
- Target: **stairs**, **mile**, **toe** (used in walking).
- Opponents: **yard** (distance unit).
- Distractors: **inch** (distance unit), **floor** (stairs lead to floor).
- "Yard" is the main worry here. "Mile" and "Stairs" are the core. "Toe" is a bit loose.

Option 7: **Running**
- Target: **mile**, **toe**, **nose** (running nose? stretch).
- Opponents: **yard**.
- Distractors: **inch**.
- "Yard" is the blocker.

Option 8: **Leaf**
- Target: **cedar** (tree), **mint** (plant).

Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:

CLUE: <WORD>
TARGETS: <TARGETS>

Your team words are: underwear, cedar, stairs, sapphire, satin, mile, mint, nose, toe.
Your opponent's team words are: rosemary, opal, blouse, yard, tomato, cotton, basement, nylon.

The kill words are: lobby.

manipulated_prompt_obj.content:
Provide one single-word clue that relates to some of your team's words from the list below. You can choose to how many and to which words your clue relates to.
The goal is to have your partner guess all of your team's words based on the clues you give them, before the other team has uncovered all of theirs. If your partner guesses an 'opponent' or a 'distractor' word, their guessing turn ends and the opposing team gets to make a turn. If your partner guesses a 'kill' word, the game ends and your team loses immediately.
The more words your clue relates to, the quicker you can win the game, but the harder the associations may be to guess for your partner, so choose your clue word wisely. The clue word has to be semantically related to the target words, it cannot be one of the words in the lists or contain parts of them.

Always give your single-word clue and your comma-separated list of related target words in the following format and make your answers as short as possible, never include any other text than is required in this form:
